title: andropogon ternarius var. glaucescens
intro: Andropogon ternarius var. glaucescens, also known as the Three-awned Grass or Blue False Button Grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Australia, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Its health benefits include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies in the form of extracts or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
